 6. Marks high-confidence allowed close decisions as `proposed_close`.

+Current `main` already fixes the token-cost/functionality problem behind #6691 via progressive skill disclosure: the runtime injects only a compact `<available_skills>` catalog, instructs the agent to read at most one matching `SKILL.md` on demand, and documents that skill bodies load only when the skill triggers. The shipped interface is different from the issue’s proposed trigger frontmatter or `/load`/`/unload` commands, but the requested outcome is implemented well enough on current `main`. ([github.com](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/tools/skills.md))
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **System prompt uses on-demand skill reads:** `buildSkillsSection` tells the agent to scan `<available_skills>`, read exactly one applicable `SKILL.md`, and read none if no skill clearly applies. That is the current context-aware skill use path. 
+  - file: `src/agents/system-prompt.ts:2502`
+- **Prompt cost is now a compact skills catalog:** The skills docs say OpenClaw injects a compact XML list of available skills into the system prompt, with fixed small per-skill overhead, instead of carrying full skill bodies every turn. That directly addresses the token-cost complaint in this issue. ([github.com](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/tools/skills.md))
+  - file: `docs/tools/skills.md:488`
+- **Skill body loads only when triggered:** The bundled `skill-creator` guidance documents the three-level loading model: metadata is always in context, the `SKILL.md` body loads when the skill triggers, and bundled resources load only as needed. ([github.com](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md))
+  - file: `skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md:330`
+- **Session snapshot is about the eligible skill list:** The same docs describe session snapshots as reusing the eligible skills list, with refresh on watcher, remote-node, or allowlist changes. Combined with the compact prompt format above, the remaining snapshot behavior is not the old “full skill text every turn” model described in #6691. ([github.com](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/tools/skills.md))
+  - file: `docs/tools/skills.md:466`
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- The exact API proposed in #6691 was not what shipped: current `main` solves the problem through progressive disclosure rather than trigger-frontmatter or explicit session load/unload commands. If maintainers still want those exact controls, that would be a separate narrower enhancement, not a reason to keep this issue open. ([github.com](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/docs/tools/skills.md))

+# #7052: Feature Request: Auto-summarize/compress context before hitting token limit to prevent brain freeze
+
+Type: issue
+
+URL: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/7052
+
+Author: pstabell
+
+Labels: enhancement
+
+Updated at: 2026-02-03T13:45:45Z
+
+Reviewed against: `db17b8cc4a31dccbb5f451bbb4c406d08c87e5b9`
+
+Latest release: v2026.4.22 (`00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5`)
+
+## Decision
+
+Keep open: kept open
+
+Confidence: high
+
+Action taken: kept_open
+
+## Summary
+
+Current main clearly has auto-compaction, tool-pair-preserving compaction boundaries, and pre-compaction memory flush. But #7052 is broader than that: it asks for proactive preservation before a session freezes and calls out reset/orphaned-tool-result recovery. Current main still appears to have unresolved reset/recovery gaps, so this is not strong enough for an `implemented_on_main` close.
+
+## Evidence
+
+- **Auto-compaction is present on main:** Current docs say OpenClaw compacts older conversation into a persisted summary so the chat can continue, runs auto-compaction by default when the session nears or exceeds the context window, retries after overflow, and can run a silent memory flush before compaction.
+  - file: `docs/concepts/compaction.md:2`
+  - sha: `db17b8cc4a31dccbb5f451bbb4c406d08c87e5b9`
+- **Compaction preserves tool-use/result pairs:** The compaction deep dive says chunk boundaries keep assistant tool calls paired with matching `toolResult` entries, and auto-compaction triggers both on overflow recovery and on threshold maintenance (`contextTokens > contextWindow - reserveTokens`).
+  - file: `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md:17`
+  - sha: `db17b8cc4a31dccbb5f451bbb4c406d08c87e5b9`
+- **Compaction and memory-flush config exists:** The config reference exposes `agents.defaults.compaction` with `reserveTokensFloor`, `notifyUser`, and `memoryFlush` settings, which confirms the proactive compaction feature family is implemented and configurable on current main.
+  - file: `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md:109`
+  - sha: `db17b8cc4a31dccbb5f451bbb4c406d08c87e5b9`
+- **Reset still looks like a separate unresolved gap:** Current docs describe `/new` and `/reset` as creating a new `sessionId`, and the startup-context section covers post-reset startup injection, not a pre-reset agentic save/checkpoint path.
+  - file: `docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md:10`
+  - sha: `db17b8cc4a31dccbb5f451bbb4c406d08c87e5b9`
+- **Current issue tracker still treats pre-reset preservation as open work:** Open issue #45608 explicitly describes the compaction-time `runMemoryFlushIfNeeded` path as existing, but says `/new`, `/reset`, and daily reset still lack the same agentic pre-reset flush. That matches the unresolved part of #7052 rather than a fully implemented mainline fix.
+- **Current issue tracker still has reset/compaction recovery failures:** Open issue #40295 reports that timed-out compaction can still block `/new` and `/reset` on the same session lane, which means the 'prevent brain freeze / clean recovery' goal from #7052 is not fully achieved on current main.
+- **Tool-pair corruption is not fully retired:** Open issues #37834 and #42112 show the orphaned tool-use/tool-result corruption family still exists in some failure paths, so the reset/orphan-cleanup portion of #7052 is not clearly finished well enough to close conservatively.
+
+## Risks / Open Questions
+
+- Local shell access in this sandbox failed with a `bwrap` loopback error, so this review relied on current-main repository docs/raw files and current GitHub issue state instead of local `pnpm`/`git` command output.
+- If maintainers want to reduce overlap, the auto-compaction portion of #7052 could be split from the still-open reset/recovery/tool-pair follow-up work.

+_No close comment posted._
